text: The Chromatic addon consumes the Chromatic GraphQL API and invokes chromatic/node rather than implementing local capture and diffing.
The official Storybook workflow runs visual tests from Storybook, marks changed stories, displays pixel changes, and accepts new baselines.
Storybook addons separate manager UI from preview annotations and preset behavior.
storybook-visual-regression combines a Storybook panel with a sidecar server, spawned JSON-RPC CLI, channel events, and SSE fallback.
storybook-visual-regression performs a fresh update capture instead of promoting the exact candidate the reviewer inspected.
Creevey separates committed baselines from report artifacts and approval copies the selected actual image into the baseline directory.
Creevey uses a typed start, stop, status, approve, approve-all, and incremental-update protocol with several comparison views.
Storybook Addon Playwright stores screenshots in __screenshots__ beside the story source file.
Storybook Addon Playwright builds a story URL with args and globals, waits for Storybook rendering, and captures through Playwright.
Playwright screenshots support animation disabling, masks, full-page capture, CSS-pixel scaling, injected styles, and PNG output.
Lost Pixel discovers Storybook stories and provides a CLI-oriented open-source engine rather than an in-Storybook review surface.
Loki is Storybook-specific and recommends Chrome in Docker to improve reproducibility across host operating systems.
reg-suit separates actual, expected, and diff directories and compares them with thresholds, anti-alias options, and bounded concurrency.
